page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier for most compute and memory operations. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1866.99 and a multi-core score of 30903.16, while Geekbench records a multi-core score of 12613.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 152.43 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations and 69.06 GB/sec for large-block write operations. Notably, the instance demonstrates strong performance in small-model LLM text generation, reaching 297.4 tokens/sec with a 135M parameter model, though performance scales down to an average 18.36 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.u1-c1m4.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides a stable foundation for applications that require consistent CPU allocation without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. This instance is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 26.9 GB/sec, database operations, Redis caching deployments, and moderate software compilation tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate solid mid-tier performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 12613.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 34102.79. It exhibits strong performance in text generation for small LLM models, achieving 297.4 tokens/sec. This instance provides a cost-effective, resource-dense profile for general-purpose enterprise applications, including static web serving, database operations, and Redis caching.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u1-c1m4.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 128 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced performance for enterprise workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring thirty-two dedicated Intel Xeon cores and one hundred twenty-eight gigabytes of system memory.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores at 2.5 GHz with x86_64 architecture
128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Strong text generation performance of 297.4 tokens/sec on small LLM models
Suited for static web serving, database operations, and Redis caching
